---
title: "story_designer"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd) | [View source](https://codeberg.org/usrbinr/stwd/src/branch/main/R/story_designer.R#L30)

# `story_designer()`

Interactive Story Layout Designer

## Usage

```r
story_designer(
  plot = NULL,
  title = "**Your {#E69F00 title} here**",
  subtitle = "Supporting context for your visualization",
  narrative = "**KEY INSIGHT:**\nYour narrative here.",
  caption = "SOURCE: Your data source"
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `plot` | Optional ggplot object to include in the preview. If NULL, a placeholder chart is used. |
| `title` | Initial title text. |
| `subtitle` | Initial subtitle text. |
| `narrative` | Initial narrative text. |
| `caption` | Initial caption text. |

## Value

Launches a Shiny app. When you click "Copy Code", the
generated `story_layout()` call is copied to your clipboard.

## Description

Launches a Shiny app to interactively design story layouts with
real-time clipping detection. Helps you find the right height
allocations before exporting.

## Examples

```r
## Not run: 
# Launch with defaults
story_designer()

# Launch with your plot
library(ggplot2)
p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) + geom_col()
story_designer(plot = p, title = "My Title")

## End(Not run)
```
